codeReview.summary: The scaffold establishes the crate tree and basic harnesses, but two foundation problems remain: the multipart inspection helper is text-only and the public module tree exposes an unsupported `skills` family that is not part of the documented architecture.

issues.description: `parse_multipart` converts the entire body to UTF-8 text and later reconstructs part bodies from `raw_body.as_bytes()`. Any non-UTF8 upload fixture will either fail to parse or lose byte fidelity, which breaks the shared multipart inspection harness this feature is supposed to provide for later file/image/audio tests.

The scaffold publishes a `skills()` resource handle even though `.factory/library/architecture.md`'s resource-family inventory does not include a Skills API. Because this feature explicitly promises a module tree aligned to the architecture, exporting an extra unsupported family leaves the public scaffold out of contract from the start.

The feature-flag wiring is only partial: `reqwest` is permanently compiled with `rustls-tls` and `blocking`, while the declared `native-tls`, `realtime`, and `webhooks` crate features do not control dependency or module selection yet. The crate still builds, but the advertised feature matrix is misleading for a publishable scaffold.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill requires `brainstorming` for shared public API changes, but there is no explicit Exec Mode fallback for the brainstorming approval gate, so workers are forced to deviate whenever the session is non-interactive.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-rust/.factory/skills/sdk-core-worker/SKILL.md:16,24 requires `brainstorming`; the handoff for worker session 0033b5d0-e8f1-4667-82ef-312db42a947d records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure = false` and a `brainstorming approval gate` deviation at /Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/beaa247f-5975-4da4-acc2-19deef388841/handoffs/2026-04-05T14-32-30-027Z__crate-scaffold-and-validator-harness__0033b5d0-e8f1-4667-82ef-312db42a947d.json.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, worker skill, and commit 107b108. The scaffold mostly matches the intended project layout, but I found two blocking contract issues: the multipart test helper cannot preserve arbitrary binary upload bodies, and the public client/module tree exposes an undocumented `skills` family that is not part of the architecture.
